project.name: Dash Security Audit Platform
project.description: AI-powered npm package security audit platform built on Dash blockchain. Developers pay with Dash for automated security audits of npm packages, with results stored on-chain via Dash Platform.

inspiration.prize_won: ENS - Most Creative Use of ENS (3rd place)

tech_stack_used.frontend: React + Vite + Tailwind CSS + Zustand + CodeMirror 6
tech_stack_used.llm: Gemini 2.5 Flash (OpenAI-compatible API), switchable to Anthropic
tech_stack_used.payment: Solidity smart contract on 0G Galileo Testnet + WalletConnect v2
tech_stack_used.cli: TypeScript, published as npmguard-cli on npm

architecture.problem: npm ecosystem has massive supply chain security problem. Malicious packages get published constantly — typosquats, hijacked accounts, credential exfiltration during postinstall. No automated, verifiable pre-install audit exists.
architecture.solution: Autonomous AI audit pipeline that detects new npm versions, runs multi-phase security analysis, publishes verifiable results on-chain.

entry_points.name: Chainlink CRE Cron
entry_points.description: Automated monitoring every 5 min. Fetches npm registry, reads ENS on-chain (trustless via EVMClient), triggers audit if not yet audited. Free, authenticated via X-API-Key.

entry_points.name: CLI On-Demand
entry_points.description: Developer runs npx npmguard-cli install <pkg>. Checks ENS for existing audit. If none, pays 0.01 0G via private key or WalletConnect QR. Engine verifies payment on-chain, runs pipeline, publishes results.

entry_points.name: Frontend Dashboard
entry_points.description: User enters package name on React dashboard. POST /audit/stream returns auditId. Frontend connects via SSE for real-time streaming of every phase.

audit_pipeline_phases.description: Downloads npm tarball, decompresses, stores in temp directory
Parses package.json, classifies files (detects binaries via magic bytes), runs dealbreaker checks (curl|sh, wget|bash, missing install scripts)
LLM (Claude Haiku) scores every source file for risk: NETWORK, FILESYSTEM, EVAL, ENV_VARS, PROCESS_SPAWN. Aggregates into 0-10 risk score. If score < 3, returns SAFE immediately.
audit_pipeline_phases.timeout: Scales with file count (base 2min, +50% per 20 extra files, max 4x)

audit_pipeline_phases.description: Only runs if triage risk >= 3. Agentic LLM (Claude Sonnet) with tools: readFile, listFiles, searchFiles, evalJs, requireAndTrace, runLifecycleHook, fastForwardTimers. Runs inside Docker sandbox (no network, read-only, 512MB RAM, 1 CPU).
Converts findings into Vitest exploit tests using MSW mocks (intercept HTTP), env stubs (NPM_TOKEN, AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID), fs spies.
Runs generated tests in Docker sandbox. Up to 3 retries per failed test. Marks proofs as TEST_CONFIRMED or TEST_UNCONFIRMED.

post_pipeline.ipfs: Uploads source tarball + report JSON to Pinata, returns CID
post_pipeline.ens: Writes text records to {version}.{package}.npmguard.eth on Sepolia: verdict, score, capabilities, report_cid, source_cid, date

bga.name: Blockchain for Good Alliance (BGA)
bga.mission: Harness blockchain technology to tackle critical global challenges. Non-profit initiative aligned with UN Sustainable Development Goals.
judging_criteria: SDG Alignment (must align with one or more UN SDGs)
Realistic execution plan and team capability
Long-term viability (not dependent on one-time funding)
qualifying_themes: Digital Public Goods (governance, digital identity, transparent data)
Finance for Inclusion (unbanked populations, cross-border remittances)
Sustainability and Climate (carbon tracking, supply chain, ESG)
example_winners: Token Tails — converts digital participation into continuous funding for animal shelters (1st, $30K)
Rumsan — blockchain for humanitarian aid distribution (2nd, $20K)
bga.fit_assessment: WEAK — security audit tool doesn't align with SDGs. Don't target this track.

dcai.name: DCAI (by Dynachain)

judging_criteria.main_goal: Encourage creative applications using DCAI ecosystem tools
judging_criteria.blockchain_requirement: Must integrate at least one interaction with their L3 on Base (API, data access, or ecosystem interaction)
judging_criteria.focus: Innovation and creativity rather than strict blockchain implementation
judging_criteria.minimum_requirement: Use at least one API or interaction from the L3 network

technical_details.reality: DCAI is an ERC-20 token on Base (chain ID 8453). Despite 'L3' marketing, there is NO actual L3 rollup chain — just smart contracts on Base mainnet.

developer_ecosystem.sdk: None — no public SDK, no developer API, no testnet

dcai.fit_assessment: VALID but SHALLOW — the only realistic integration is accepting DCAI as payment on Base. No SDK, no API, no testnet. Integration would be cosmetic.

dash.name: DASH

judging_criteria.description: Build real-world applications using Dash — fast, low-cost payments + Web3 platform (Drive & DAPI).

platform_overview.what_is_dash_platform: Web3 technology stack built as Layer 2 on Dash payment network. Transforms masternode network into decentralized cloud.
platform_overview.drive: Decentralized storage on masternode network. Built on GroveDB (hierarchical authenticated database with cryptographic proofs for any query). Stores documents validated against data contracts.
platform_overview.dapi: Decentralized API exposing JSON-RPC and gRPC endpoints. Unified access to Layer 1 (Core) and Layer 2 (Platform). Runs on masternode infrastructure. TLS encrypted. Returns cryptographic proofs.
platform_overview.dpns: Dash Platform Name Service — human-readable names (like ENS but native to Dash).
platform_overview.instantsend: Sub-2-second transaction finality. LLMQ quorum of masternodes approve and lock transaction. Automatic for all transactions, no extra fee.
platform_overview.chainlocks: Every 12 hours, 300-400 masternode quorum finalizes blocks. Prevents 51% attacks.

technical_details.data_contracts: JSON Schema-based (not Solidity). Define document types with properties, indices. Backward-compatible versioning. Any app can read any other app's contracts.

javascript.package: npm install dash (legacy) or npm install @dashevo/evo-sdk (newer)
javascript.capabilities: Wallet ops, identity management, data contract registration, document CRUD
javascript.note: Does NOT support proof verification — use only with trusted nodes in production

rust.note: Recommended for production. Full proof verification support.

dash.fit_assessment: STRONGEST FIT — 'AI + crypto tools' is a listed category. Deep integration possible: DPNS for package naming, Drive for audit storage, InstantSend for payments, Data Contracts for on-chain results, DAPI for trustless queries. JS SDK available.

reasoning: AI + crypto tools is explicitly listed as a project type
Dash Platform provides deep infrastructure (Drive, DAPI, DPNS, Data Contracts) — not just a payment token
JS/TS SDK available — matches existing TypeScript codebase
Testnet with faucet available for development
InstantSend (<2s payments) is a strong demo moment
Data contracts (JSON Schema) replace the need for Solidity — faster to build in hackathon
DPNS can replace ENS subnames for package naming
Drive can replace IPFS for audit report storage

ens_subnames_sepolia.why: Same concept, native to Dash, no external dependency

ipfs_via_pinata.why: Store audit reports as data contract documents, no Pinata/IPFS needed

solidity_payment_contract.why: No Solidity needed, just JSON — faster to build

chainlink_cre_monitoring.replace_with: Custom cron or keep similar approach
chainlink_cre_monitoring.why: Chainlink is not part of Dash ecosystem, simplify to a cron job

key_differentiators_from_npmguard: InstantSend payment UX (<2s confirmation in demo)
No Solidity — data contracts in JSON Schema
Different target audience or scope angle needed to avoid looking like a copy
warnings: Don't just copy NpmGuard — judges can see it. Need a meaningfully different angle.
Don't try to hit both DCAI and DASH tracks with one project — neither integration will be deep enough.
'Top up tokens to pay for audits' alone is a thin integration. Use Drive, DAPI, DPNS deeply.
BGA track does not fit — security audit tool doesn't align with UN SDGs.
